title: Pseudocyclosorus ciliatus
taxonomy: Pseudocyclosorus ciliatus belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. It is classified in the order Polypodiales, a division of vascular plants that includes most modern ferns. Within the order Polypodiales, Pseudocyclosorus ciliatus is further classified in the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or autumn ferns. The genus Pseudocyclosorus is a genus of ferns within the family Thelypteridaceae. At the kingdom level, Pseudocyclosorus ciliatus is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants.

intro: Pseudocyclosorus ciliatus, also known as the Chinese hook fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The Chinese hook fern has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Pseudocyclosorus ciliatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
